title: Normalized Feed Domain, Content Variants, and Provenance · Language Lens: Feed Go Loss Report
summary: RSS and Atom transport structs leak into storage, missing data is invented, HTML and text are conflated, source identifiers are discarded, and updates cannot be explained. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go loss report increment in loss-aware normalized feed and entry model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Emit field-level admitted, defaulted, dropped, conflicted, and unsupported evidence for each normalization run.
Normalize instants while retaining raw date, precision, timezone, language, and base-URI provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Loss Report · Theory Feed Go Loss Report
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 6556 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile format-specific source values with normalized identity, content kinds, and loss evidence; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Emit field-level admitted, defaulted, dropped, conflicted, and unsupported evidence for each normalization run.
